message:
test(core): recorder stack integration tests — storage + retention + telemetry + audit (wa-4c9f)

26 cross-module integration tests exercising:
- Storage append → telemetry metrics recording
- Retention sweep → audit event generation with hash chain
- Sensitivity tier classification → retention → audit pipeline
- Access control decisions (allow/deny/elevate) → audit trail
- Telemetry snapshot → diagnostic summary
- Full pipeline: append → flush → telemetry → retention → audit
- Audit log resume across persistence boundaries
- Tamper detection (modified/deleted/inserted entries)
- Checkpoint holds preventing premature purge
- Multi-actor concurrent operations audit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-ewt0","title":"FTS maintenance: verify/rebuild command","description":"## What
Provide a CLI command to verify and rebuild FTS indexes with progress.

## Why
Users need a safe recovery path when search results are inconsistent.

## How
- `wa search fts verify` checks row counts and index integrity
- `wa search fts rebuild` runs a rebuild with progress output

## Success Criteria
- Commands are safe and idempotent
- Progress is visible and redacted","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:54.401466312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.279084-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:52:31.046183555Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-ewt0","depends_on_id":"wa-wvw7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-ezxl","title":"Implement mute/unmute (event key) + listing muted items","description":"# Task: Implement mute/unmute for events

## Goal
Give users explicit control over noisy events without losing visibility.

## Requirements
- Commands:
  - `wa mute <event_key> --for <duration>`
  - `wa unmute <event_key>`
  - `wa muted` (list current mutes)
- Scoping:
  - workspace-scoped by default
  - optional global scope (explicit flag)
- Storage:
  - persist mutes with TTL

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - TTL expiry
  - scope enforcement
- Output tests:
  - JSON schema for list

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can mute noisy events without hiding critical issues.

 {"id":"wa-f0i5","title":"Diff/preview + rollback semantics","description":"## What
Provide a safe diff/preview and rollback workflow for applying profiles.

## Why
Users must understand changes before switching to avoid surprises.

## How
- `wa config profile diff <name>` shows changes
- `wa config profile apply <name> --dry-run` shows preview
- Store last applied profile for rollback

## Success Criteria
- Preview output is stable and redacted
- Rollback restores previous config without loss","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltGlen","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:06:47.423121303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.238737-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-05T09:03:16.942990832Z","close_reason":"Implemented config profile diff/apply/rollback with manifest updates and backup/rollback flow","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-f0i5","depends_on_id":"wa-wo94","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-f0i5","depends_on_id":"wa-ts1a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-f5wn","title":"FTUI-03.1 Introduce terminal session ownership abstraction aligned with ftui","description":"## Background\nCurrent TUI setup/teardown is tied to crossterm/ratatui lifecycle. We need a migration-safe ownership abstraction that matches ftui terminal session discipline.\n\n## Deliverables\n- terminal session lifecycle interface\n- raw mode / alt-screen / cleanup ownership model\n- integration points for command handoff and return\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- session ownership is singular and testable\n- teardown guarantees are explicit and verified.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:49.283003308Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.272674-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:27:27.521835436Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-f5wn","depends_on_id":"wa-53q9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-f6j9","title":"Implement Playwright auth flow for Google (Gemini CLI /auth)","description":"# Task: Implement Playwright auth flow for Google (Gemini CLI `/auth`)

## Goal
Implement `BrowserAutomation::complete_google_auth(...)` that establishes an authenticated Google session suitable for Gemini CLI.

## Context / why this is hard
- Google sign-in is complex (SSO, MFA, device prompts).
- Automation can be rate-limited or challenged.
- Our priority is **robustness and safety**, not full automation at any cost.

## Expected workflow integration
Gemini usage-limit workflow will:
1. send `/auth` into the Gemini CLI pane
2. parse any printed instructions (URL/code) if present
3. call this Playwright flow
4. return to the pane and resume/restart session as appropriate

## Implementation outline
### Happy path (already authenticated)
- Load profile for `(service=google, account_key)`.
- Navigate to a stable page (`https://accounts.google.com/`).
- Detect a "signed-in" selector (account avatar / profile) and return `Success`.

### Login path
- If not signed in, proceed through:
  - email entry
  - account selection
  - (potentially) password/MFA — which we do NOT automate

### Password/MFA/SSO handling
- If any step requires password/MFA/SSO confirmation:
  - return `NeedsHuman` with URL and instructions (redacted)
  - keep the browser visible (non-headless) for manual completion
  - after completion, persist profile storage state

## Safety requirements
- Strict timeouts for waits.
- Redact sensitive URL parameters.
- Do not store secrets in DB.

## Testing
- Integration tests (offline):
  - Playwright against local HTML fixtures representing:
    - already-authenticated state
    - needs-human state (password/MFA)
  - validate structured outcomes and redaction
- Manual smoke tests:
  - validate on a real machine with a pre-authenticated profile

## Deliverables
- Google auth flow implementation using persistent profiles.
- A clear "manual completion" fallback path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- With a pre-authenticated profile, flow returns `Success` reliably.
- Without a profile, flow returns `NeedsHuman` with actionable instructions (no secrets leaked).

 {"id":"wa-f8j1","title":"Integrate workflow runner to emit/execute StepPlans (plan-first execution)","description":"# Task: Plan-first workflow execution

## Goal
Refactor workflow execution to be plan-first:
- generate StepPlans up front when possible
- execute with per-step verification + durable logging

## Requirements
- Workflow runner behavior:
  - construct an ActionPlan (StepPlans) prior to execution (or incrementally with explicit boundaries)
  - persist the plan before performing side effects
  - execute steps with:
    - precondition checks
    - PolicyEngine authorization
    - verification
    - bounded retries
    - explicit failure handling
- Step log model:
  - record start/end timestamps (monotonic where possible)
  - record verification evidence (snippets, state queries)
  - record policy decisions (redacted)

## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - fixture workflow generates expected plan + step logs
  - failure in step N produces actionable error referencing step id/kind

## Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow runs are explainable via their persisted ActionPlan and step logs.
- Retrying a workflow does not double-apply side effects when idempotency keys are used.
